The anthropological definition of environment includes the physical landscape, climate, and natural resources, as well as the social structures, beliefs, values, and practices that influence human relationships with the natural world. It also acknowledges the historical and contemporary power dynamics, colonialism, and globalization that have impacted the environment and the distribution of resources.
